_meta.subtaskTitle: Read the full hadith text in Arabic and English
_meta.parentTaskTitle: Memorise the hadith of Jibril (Sahih Muslim 8) on Islam, Iman, and Ihsan
_meta.claudeScholarQuery: For a MILOS subtask titled 'Read the full hadith text in Arabic and English' under the parent task 'Memorise the hadith of Jibril (Sahih Muslim 8) on Islam, Iman, and Ihsan' (module: faith_shahada_core), propose exactly 2 NotebookLM queries to a Quran/Hadith primary-source corpus: (1) Quran-biased — name likely primary ayat that directly or contextually anchor this subtask; (2) Hadith-biased — name likely Sahih hadith (Bukhari/Muslim preferred) that directly or contextually anchor this subtask. Return the queries inside a <queries></queries> block as a numbered list, each one sentence, ready to paste into a retrieval system. Do NOT answer the queries — query formulation only.

muslimScholarQueries.query: What are the primary ayat, such as Surah An-Najm 53:3-4 and Surah Al-Hashr 59:7, that establish the divine inspiration and binding authority of the Prophet's teachings, contextually anchoring the necessity of reading and preserving his exact words?

Which authentic narrations, specifically the primary text found in Sahih Muslim 8 and its variant in Sahih al-Bukhari 50, provide the complete Arabic and English transcriptions of the Hadith of Jibril detailing Islam, Iman, and Ihsan?

sources.ref: Sahih Muslim 8 (Book 1, Hadith of Jibril via Umar ibn al-Khattab)
sources.translation: A man dressed in pure white clothes... knelt before the Apostle and said: Muhammad, inform me about al-Islam. The Messenger of Allah said: Al-Islam implies that you testify that there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad is the messenger of Allah, and you establish prayer, pay Zakat, observe the fast of Ramadan, and perform pilgrimage to the House if you are solvent enough... Inform me about Iman. He replied: That you affirm your faith in Allah, in His angels, in His Books, in His Apostles, in the Day of Judgment, and you affirm your faith in the Divine Decree about good and evil... Inform me about al-Ihsan. He said: That you worship Allah as if you are seeing Him, for though you don't see Him, He, verily, sees you... He was Gabriel (the angel). He came to you in order to instruct you in matters of religion.
sources.rationale: This IS the hadith the subtask names — the full English matn of the Hadith of Jibril as narrated by Umar. The subtask asks the user to read this hadith in full; the entry supplies exactly that text. Bayyinah (Sahih Muslim primary narration) + direct. Arabic omitted per MS disclaimer: the corpus explicitly stated 'the provided sources do not contain the Arabic transcriptions of the Hadith of Jibril.'

notebookTrace.query: Which authentic narrations, specifically the primary text found in Sahih Muslim 8 and its variant in Sahih al-Bukhari 50, provide the complete Arabic and English transcriptions of the Hadith of Jibril detailing Islam, Iman, and Ihsan?

sources.ref: Sahih Bukhari, Volume 1, Book 2, Number 47 (parallel narration via Abu Huraira)
sources.translation: One day while the Prophet was sitting in the company of some people, (the angel) Gabriel came and asked, 'What is faith?' Allah's Messenger replied, 'Faith is to believe in Allah, His angels, (the) meeting with Him, His Apostles, and to believe in Resurrection.' Then he further asked, 'What is Islam?' Allah's Messenger replied, 'To worship Allah alone and none else, to offer prayers perfectly, to pay the compulsory charity (Zakat) and to observe fasts during the month of Ramadan.' Then he further asked, 'What is Ihsan (perfection)?' Allah's Messenger replied, 'To worship Allah as if you see Him, and if you cannot achieve this state of devotion then you must consider that He is looking at you.'... That was Gabriel who came to teach the people their religion.
sources.rationale: The Bukhari parallel narration of the same encounter via Abu Huraira. A reader doing the subtask benefits from having both canonical narrations side-by-side to read the full hadith in its two primary forms. Contextual rather than direct because the subtask's named target is Muslim 8; Bukhari 47/50 is the companion. Bayyinah (Sahih Bukhari primary). Arabic omitted per the same MS disclaimer.

notebookTrace.query: Which authentic narrations, specifically the primary text found in Sahih Muslim 8 and its variant in Sahih al-Bukhari 50, provide the complete Arabic and English transcriptions of the Hadith of Jibril detailing Islam, Iman, and Ihsan?

_groundedBarCheck.reason: 2 hadith entries; both Bayyinah (Sahih Muslim + Sahih Bukhari); 1 direct + 1 contextual. Bar met. Quran axis omitted: q1 returned 53:3-4 / 59:7 / 4:80 only via paraphrase with cited_text pointing to surah headers and unrelated fragments — no tight verse-level binding achievable. The hadith axis alone carries the bar and is precisely on-topic for this subtask.
